state.summary: All three datasets were created and populate ran, but each run FAILED partway. Partial rows were salvaged to the export files above. See blocker below.

blocker.detail: The OpenRouter API key in .env.local has a per-key spend limit of $10 and is exhausted (usage=$9.96, limit_remaining=$0.04). Populate returns HTTP 402: 'This request requires more credits, or fewer max_tokens'. The parent account still has ~$9.5 of its $25 balance, but THIS key is capped at $10 and cannot reach it.
fix_options: Preferred: raise/remove the $10 limit on this key (or mint a new uncapped key) at https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys, then re-run the populate step below.

Also required for the cheap default model: BigSet's default populate model is qwen/qwen3.7-max, which this account's data policy currently BLOCKS ('No endpoints available matching your guardrail restrictions and data policy'). Either (a) allow those providers at https://openrouter.ai/settings/privacy so the cheaper qwen default works, or (b) keep the Anthropic override shown below (works but ~10x more expensive, which is what drained the key).

runner.tool: local BigSet via `npx @adamexu/bigset` (no Docker; backend on 127.0.0.1:3501, workspace bigset-local)
runner.credentials: TINYFISH_API_KEY + OPENROUTER_API_KEY were stored into the OS keychain (service ai.bigset.local-credentials, accounts bigset-local:tinyfish / bigset-local:openrouter). They persist, so future runs do not need re-seeding.
runner.model_override_used: POPULATE_ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 and INVESTIGATE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 (needed because the qwen default is blocked by the account data policy).

start_backend_command: cd ~/side-projects/tasco-ai-map && set -a && source .env.local && set +a && BIGSET_LOCAL_WORKSPACE_ID=bigset-local BIGSET_KEYCHAIN_PORT=3599 POPULATE_ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL=z-ai/glm-5.2 INVESTIGATE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=z-ai/glm-5.2 npx @adamexu/bigset start

repopulate_commands._comment: After fixing the OpenRouter key limit, start the backend (command above), then re-run populate for each dataset:

export_commands._comment: Backend must be running. `rows --json` prints all stored rows to stdout.

model_commandment: USER MANDATE (final): run populate with POPULATE_ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL=z-ai/glm-5.2 and INVESTIGATE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=z-ai/glm-5.2 (text-to-text is fine for the orchestrator). If OpenRouter returns the data-policy 404 that hit qwen, allow the provider at https://openrouter.ai/settings/privacy first.
